benchmark.name: Marketplace Agentic Memory Benchmark
benchmark.methodology: Public-evidence architecture benchmark. Scores are 0-5 by criterion, adjusted by source confidence. This does not claim black-box latency or accuracy for closed products without API access.

criteria.label: Memory model depth and typed tiers

summary.recommendation: Keep MemRoOS's governed multi-agent memory position; strengthen the live beta with hot-path retrieval, public recall benchmarks, and temporal fact invalidation.

ranked.name: MemRoOS beta live
ranked.shortAssessment: Live beta architecture wins on governed multi-agent memory after passing the full recall gate.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: Live beta adds public marketplace scoring, failure fixtures, and passing full recall reports to the existing eval substrate.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Live beta keeps governed memory as the primary wedge and adds policy-backed memory promotion/retention.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Live beta adds temporal invalidation, hot summary/context blocks, and entity/version reconciliation on top of existing tiers.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: Live beta keeps A2A/REST/MCP/LangGraph/HIL and makes retrieved context packs observable per run.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: Live beta adds explicit tenant isolation, export, retention, and compliance checklist docs.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: Live beta introduces hot context cache, async consolidation, tier fan-out, reranking, and benchmark latency budgets.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: Live beta ties every retrieval to eval traces, drift checks, and operator-approved memory self-edits.

portability_open_exits.rationale: Live beta keeps source-controlled, self-hostable, framework-neutral interfaces.

details.rationale: Live beta adds public marketplace scoring, failure fixtures, and passing full recall reports to the existing eval substrate.
Live beta keeps governed memory as the primary wedge and adds policy-backed memory promotion/retention.
Live beta adds temporal invalidation, hot summary/context blocks, and entity/version reconciliation on top of existing tiers.
Live beta keeps A2A/REST/MCP/LangGraph/HIL and makes retrieved context packs observable per run.
Live beta adds explicit tenant isolation, export, retention, and compliance checklist docs.
Live beta introduces hot context cache, async consolidation, tier fan-out, reranking, and benchmark latency budgets.
Live beta ties every retrieval to eval traces, drift checks, and operator-approved memory self-edits.
Live beta keeps source-controlled, self-hostable, framework-neutral interfaces.

ranked.name: MemRoOS prior baseline
ranked.shortAssessment: Strongest governance-plus-workflow shape, but public benchmark proof and hot-path retrieval need to catch up.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: Has seeded recall evals with recall, precision, MRR, tier coverage, false-positive rate, and latency, but no external LOCOMO/DMR/LongMemEval-style benchmark yet.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Operator auth, per-agent write paths, audit-like episodic rows, dispatch governance, and security/planning surfaces are central to the product.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Vector, graph, episodic, qmd, knowledge, and skill surfaces create a deeper operating memory model than pure conversation stores.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: A2A, REST shim, MCP, LangGraph delegation, HIL, skills, and registry make memory part of agent operations rather than a detached API.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: Self-hosted/private-network-first posture is strong; enterprise compliance packaging remains immature.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: Has caching/performance work, but current eval path is not marketed around sub-10ms memory reads or GPU/cache economics.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: Eval engine, SEAL loop, policy lab, ledger, flow, and dashboard concepts are a clear differentiator.

portability_open_exits.rationale: Source-controlled, framework-agnostic interfaces and local-first posture give strong exits.

details.rationale: Has seeded recall evals with recall, precision, MRR, tier coverage, false-positive rate, and latency, but no external LOCOMO/DMR/LongMemEval-style benchmark yet.
Operator auth, per-agent write paths, audit-like episodic rows, dispatch governance, and security/planning surfaces are central to the product.
Vector, graph, episodic, qmd, knowledge, and skill surfaces create a deeper operating memory model than pure conversation stores.
A2A, REST shim, MCP, LangGraph delegation, HIL, skills, and registry make memory part of agent operations rather than a detached API.
Self-hosted/private-network-first posture is strong; enterprise compliance packaging remains immature.
Has caching/performance work, but current eval path is not marketed around sub-10ms memory reads or GPU/cache economics.
Eval engine, SEAL loop, policy lab, ledger, flow, and dashboard concepts are a clear differentiator.
Source-controlled, framework-agnostic interfaces and local-first posture give strong exits.

ranked.name: Letta
ranked.shortAssessment: Deep agent memory and learning architecture; closer to stateful agents than an enterprise memory control plane.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: Letta has memory-first architecture and public eval/leaderboard surfaces.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Persistent state, runs/steps, tools, and RBAC docs exist, but governed enterprise memory is not the main wedge.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Core memory blocks, shared blocks, archival memory, recall memory, messages, tools, context hierarchy, and self-editing memory are central.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: Stateful agent runs/steps, tools, MCP, HIL, multi-agent patterns, and APIs are present.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: Docker server and API platform exist; full enterprise data-control posture still needs verification.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: Focus is context management quality more than latency/cache economics.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: Runs/steps, eval docs, continual learning, background memory agents, and memory palace support strong observability.

portability_open_exits.rationale: BYO API keys, local/server options, and memory portability across models are public claims.

details.rationale: Letta has memory-first architecture and public eval/leaderboard surfaces.
Persistent state, runs/steps, tools, and RBAC docs exist, but governed enterprise memory is not the main wedge.
Core memory blocks, shared blocks, archival memory, recall memory, messages, tools, context hierarchy, and self-editing memory are central.
Stateful agent runs/steps, tools, MCP, HIL, multi-agent patterns, and APIs are present.
Docker server and API platform exist; full enterprise data-control posture still needs verification.
Focus is context management quality more than latency/cache economics.
Runs/steps, eval docs, continual learning, background memory agents, and memory palace support strong observability.
BYO API keys, local/server options, and memory portability across models are public claims.

ranked.name: Mem0 Platform
ranked.shortAssessment: Strong production memory layer and benchmark baseline; less differentiated on workflow governance.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: Public docs and paper describe production memory and benchmark evaluation; graph/entity behavior has changed in OSS v3.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Managed platform claims audit logs and workspace governance by default.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Platform has graph memory, multimodal, categories, filters, async, rerankers; OSS v3 removed explicit graph store support in favor of entity linking.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: Connectors and framework integrations are broad, including MCP, LangChain, CrewAI, and Vercel AI SDK.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: Managed and open-source paths exist; managed platform is the enterprise path.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: Production-scale managed service and new algorithm focus on fewer LLM calls and hybrid retrieval.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: Dashboard and activity management are present; full operator-approved self-improvement loop is not the core product.

portability_open_exits.rationale: OSS path helps, but platform features and graph behavior differ from self-hosted OSS.

details.rationale: Public docs and paper describe production memory and benchmark evaluation; graph/entity behavior has changed in OSS v3.
Managed platform claims audit logs and workspace governance by default.
Platform has graph memory, multimodal, categories, filters, async, rerankers; OSS v3 removed explicit graph store support in favor of entity linking.
Connectors and framework integrations are broad, including MCP, LangChain, CrewAI, and Vercel AI SDK.
Managed and open-source paths exist; managed platform is the enterprise path.
Production-scale managed service and new algorithm focus on fewer LLM calls and hybrid retrieval.
Dashboard and activity management are present; full operator-approved self-improvement loop is not the core product.
OSS path helps, but platform features and graph behavior differ from self-hosted OSS.

ranked.name: Zep
ranked.shortAssessment: Probably the strongest pure memory-quality competitor because temporal invalidation is product-native.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: Temporal KG architecture and DMR benchmark claims are public.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Context engineering primitives are strong; enterprise governance posture is less central than memory quality.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Knowledge graph, facts, entities, episodes, summaries, observations, user summaries, custom edge/entity types, and fact invalidation are explicit.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: Agentic tool use and graph/context APIs are strong, but full orchestration is not the primary product.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: Commercial platform exists; self-host/export posture needs deeper review.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: Context block assembly is optimized, but public latency/cost claims are less aggressive than cache-first competitors.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: Temporal graph facts are inspectable; eval/self-improvement workflow is not the main surface.

portability_open_exits.rationale: API abstraction helps, but KG semantics may be sticky.

details.rationale: Temporal KG architecture and DMR benchmark claims are public.
Context engineering primitives are strong; enterprise governance posture is less central than memory quality.
Knowledge graph, facts, entities, episodes, summaries, observations, user summaries, custom edge/entity types, and fact invalidation are explicit.
Agentic tool use and graph/context APIs are strong, but full orchestration is not the primary product.
Commercial platform exists; self-host/export posture needs deeper review.
Context block assembly is optimized, but public latency/cost claims are less aggressive than cache-first competitors.
Temporal graph facts are inspectable; eval/self-improvement workflow is not the main surface.
API abstraction helps, but KG semantics may be sticky.

ranked.name: AXME
ranked.shortAssessment: Strong orchestration/governance competitor; memory appears narrower and coding-agent focused.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: AI coding memory is claimed, but public memory quality benchmarks were not found.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Fleet governance, quarantine, policy guardrails, audit trail, and multi-tenancy are explicit platform claims.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Persistent coding memory is visible, but broader typed enterprise memory tiers are not the main claim.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: Durable execution, scheduling, time-travel replay, fleet observability, and open protocol are the core product.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: Public comparison claims self-host or hosted deployment and full portability.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: Durable execution and replay reduce wasted work, but memory latency proof is not public.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: Fleet-wide traces, logs, metrics, replay, quarantine, and policy visibility are strong.

portability_open_exits.rationale: Open protocol and self-host/hosted positioning are explicit.

details.rationale: AI coding memory is claimed, but public memory quality benchmarks were not found.
Fleet governance, quarantine, policy guardrails, audit trail, and multi-tenancy are explicit platform claims.
Persistent coding memory is visible, but broader typed enterprise memory tiers are not the main claim.
Durable execution, scheduling, time-travel replay, fleet observability, and open protocol are the core product.
Public comparison claims self-host or hosted deployment and full portability.
Durable execution and replay reduce wasted work, but memory latency proof is not public.
Fleet-wide traces, logs, metrics, replay, quarantine, and policy visibility are strong.
Open protocol and self-host/hosted positioning are explicit.

ranked.name: EverMind / EverMemOS
ranked.shortAssessment: Important benchmark-oriented memory competitor; needs direct verification beyond press claims.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: Press release claims SOTA accuracy across four major memory benchmarks.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Enterprise-grade security is claimed, but audit/permission mechanics were not clear in public evidence.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Episodic trace formation, semantic consolidation, and reconstructive recollection are explicit architectural primitives.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: Memory platform focus is clear; multi-agent workflow/governance integration is less visible.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: Cloud API and open-source system are claimed, but enterprise deployment specifics need verification.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: Claims higher accuracy with a fraction of full-context compute cost.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: Self-organizing memory is core, but operator eval/audit workflow details are unclear.

portability_open_exits.rationale: Open-source EverMemOS improves exit posture, assuming license and implementation are acceptable.

details.rationale: Press release claims SOTA accuracy across four major memory benchmarks.
Enterprise-grade security is claimed, but audit/permission mechanics were not clear in public evidence.
Episodic trace formation, semantic consolidation, and reconstructive recollection are explicit architectural primitives.
Memory platform focus is clear; multi-agent workflow/governance integration is less visible.
Cloud API and open-source system are claimed, but enterprise deployment specifics need verification.
Claims higher accuracy with a fraction of full-context compute cost.
Self-organizing memory is core, but operator eval/audit workflow details are unclear.
Open-source EverMemOS improves exit posture, assuming license and implementation are acceptable.

ranked.name: Tytan TAO / Cortex
ranked.shortAssessment: Most enterprise-sounding governed memory competitor, but public technical proof is thin.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: No public memory benchmark methodology found.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Public page claims SOC 2 Type II, HMAC-notarized memory, RBAC/ABAC, HIL, and complete audit trails.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Cortex is described as persistent, governed, notarized memory across six layers.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: Public page claims durable DAG workflows, model routing, agent types, tool registry, and MCP enterprise adapters.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: Enterprise posture is strong, but self-host/export details were not clear from public docs.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: No clear public latency or cost benchmark found.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: Executive intelligence, audit trails, and decision explainability are central claims.

portability_open_exits.rationale: Closed agentic OS positioning implies lock-in risk unless proven otherwise.

details.rationale: No public memory benchmark methodology found.
Public page claims SOC 2 Type II, HMAC-notarized memory, RBAC/ABAC, HIL, and complete audit trails.
Cortex is described as persistent, governed, notarized memory across six layers.
Public page claims durable DAG workflows, model routing, agent types, tool registry, and MCP enterprise adapters.
Enterprise posture is strong, but self-host/export details were not clear from public docs.
No clear public latency or cost benchmark found.
Executive intelligence, audit trails, and decision explainability are central claims.
Closed agentic OS positioning implies lock-in risk unless proven otherwise.

ranked.name: AgenticMemory.ai
ranked.shortAssessment: Likely the closest small closed competitor from the X signal: fast, simple, API-first, but less proven for governed enterprise workflows.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: Claims semantic search and auto summaries, but public benchmark methodology was not found.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Public page claims tenant-isolated spaces, scoped API keys, TTL, and no training on customer data.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Messages, threads, context, state, entities, entries, scratchpad, shared world memory, semantic search, and webhooks are documented primitives.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: REST, MCP, OpenClaw, LangChain, CrewAI, OpenAI Agents, AutoGen, Cursor, Semantic Kernel, Bedrock, and Vercel AI integrations are advertised.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: Hosted service with enterprise plan; no clear self-hosted/private-network deployment evidence found.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: Claims sub-millisecond hot-cache reads; not independently measured here.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: Usage analytics and memory events are suggested, but no eval/self-improvement control plane is visible.

portability_open_exits.rationale: REST/MCP lowers integration lock-in, but persistence is in a closed hosted service.

details.rationale: Claims semantic search and auto summaries, but public benchmark methodology was not found.
Public page claims tenant-isolated spaces, scoped API keys, TTL, and no training on customer data.
Messages, threads, context, state, entities, entries, scratchpad, shared world memory, semantic search, and webhooks are documented primitives.
REST, MCP, OpenClaw, LangChain, CrewAI, OpenAI Agents, AutoGen, Cursor, Semantic Kernel, Bedrock, and Vercel AI integrations are advertised.
Hosted service with enterprise plan; no clear self-hosted/private-network deployment evidence found.
Claims sub-millisecond hot-cache reads; not independently measured here.
Usage analytics and memory events are suggested, but no eval/self-improvement control plane is visible.
REST/MCP lowers integration lock-in, but persistence is in a closed hosted service.

ranked.name: GBrain
ranked.shortAssessment: Relevant to the X links, but not really a closed enterprise competitor yet.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: Public discussion includes high recall claims, but independent/reproducible benchmark framing is disputed.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Personal memory/knowledge brain positioning, not an enterprise governance layer.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Persistent searchable knowledge, entity detection, enrichment, and compiled truth are reported.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: Strong fit for OpenClaw/Hermes community and agent-native workflows.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: MIT/open-source posture is strong for control, but enterprise compliance is not the focus.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: Scalability concerns for enterprise-scale deployments are noted in public analysis.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: Not enough public evidence of governed eval/observability loops.

portability_open_exits.rationale: Open-source, OpenClaw/Hermes oriented setup gives strong portability.

details.rationale: Public discussion includes high recall claims, but independent/reproducible benchmark framing is disputed.
Personal memory/knowledge brain positioning, not an enterprise governance layer.
Persistent searchable knowledge, entity detection, enrichment, and compiled truth are reported.
Strong fit for OpenClaw/Hermes community and agent-native workflows.
MIT/open-source posture is strong for control, but enterprise compliance is not the focus.
Scalability concerns for enterprise-scale deployments are noted in public analysis.
Not enough public evidence of governed eval/observability loops.
Open-source, OpenClaw/Hermes oriented setup gives strong portability.

ranked.name: WorldFlow AI
ranked.shortAssessment: Performance-first memory layer; stronger threat to latency/cost narrative than to governed agentic memory.

recall_quality_evals.rationale: Public evidence emphasizes caching and acceleration more than recall accuracy benchmarks.

governed_memory_audit_permissions.rationale: Claims edge PII detection, GDPR compliance, and SOC 2 planned.

memory_model_depth_typed_multitier.rationale: Four pillars cover gateway cache, KV-cache acceleration, long-term memory, and cost optimization.

agent_workflow_integration_orchestration.rationale: Works with any LLM provider by base URL changes, but orchestration/HIL/multi-agent governance is not the main surface.

enterprise_deployment_data_control.rationale: Enterprise demo positioning is clear; deployment/control model is not deeply public.

performance_latency_cost_path.rationale: Claims sub-10ms cache hits, 2-12x TTFT speedup, and 40-70% cost reduction.

observability_self_improvement.rationale: AI observability and cost analytics are advertised.

portability_open_exits.rationale: Zero-code base URL swap helps adoption, but the platform appears closed.

details.rationale: Public evidence emphasizes caching and acceleration more than recall accuracy benchmarks.
Claims edge PII detection, GDPR compliance, and SOC 2 planned.
Four pillars cover gateway cache, KV-cache acceleration, long-term memory, and cost optimization.
Works with any LLM provider by base URL changes, but orchestration/HIL/multi-agent governance is not the main surface.
Enterprise demo positioning is clear; deployment/control model is not deeply public.
Claims sub-10ms cache hits, 2-12x TTFT speedup, and 40-70% cost reduction.
AI observability and cost analytics are advertised.
Zero-code base URL swap helps adoption, but the platform appears closed.
